reasoning: The FDIC 'National Rate' for savings accounts currently sits at 0.40% and has been slowly declining from 0.46% in early 2024, with a small bounce from 0.38% in the summer back to 0.40%. The Fed has initiated its rate-cutting cycle with cuts in September and October 2025. These consecutive federal funds rate reductions are highly likely to exert downward pressure on average savings account yields, as banks (especially high-yield offerers) adjust their deposit rates downward. Therefore, it is quite improbable that the savings rate will increase above the current 0.40% baseline by December 2025. The probability of an increase by February 2026 is similarly low due to expected further Fed rate cuts in late 2025 and early 2026, though we follow the forecasting instruction to increase uncertainty (pull probabilities closer to 0.5) for more distant horizons.
